text: with both hands and twisting, forcing him to drop the knife . “You’re all going to pay for this!” Devon pulled away from Steve’s hold. “Do you think you can defeat Warlord?” He laughed, exposing his crimson-stained teeth . Stooping, Gary picked up the knife, not looking away from Devon . “Stay the fuck away from me.” Devon retreated toward the crowd. “You’ll all be dead before — ” From all sides, the crowd took hold of Devon, grabbing fistfuls of his clothes and hair, clamping their hands on his arms and legs . “What the fuck?” he shouted. “Let go of me !” Their hold on him tightened as he thrashed, and they lifted him off his feet, forcing his arms and legs to the sides. Gary walked forward slowly. “You killed them .” “Get the fuck a—” Devon’s words were cut off by his cry of pain as Gary thrust the knife into his gut. “God, you fucking — ” The crowd was eerily silent. Gary pulled the knife free and plunged it in again, and again, and again, into Devon’s abdomen and chest, covering his hand and forearm in blood . “You stole their lives, you bastard. Took them from me, took them from me !” Devon’s eyes bulged and the tendons on his neck stood out. He lifted his bobbing head to look Gary in the eye . “I hope there’s a hell, so you can fucking burn in it,” Gary said, and slashed the knife across Devon’s throat. Blood poured from the gaping wound to pool on the dirt below . Everyone watched as Gary stepped back and Devon’s lifeless body was tossed, face-first, onto the ground. Gary’s eyes gleamed. His arm and chest were splattered with blood, shoulders rising and falling with his ragged breaths. The kind, sweet man Lara had always known was gone; if she’d done something sooner, could she have saved Gary from this ? “I’ll fight,” he said. “To the death. I want that fucking bot ended .” “Who else?” Lara asked, looking over the grim faces . “I will.” Steve took a single step forward . “ Me , too .” “ And me .” One-by-one, the crowd agreed . “How can you be sure we’ll win?” Scarlet asked. Whatever fire had driven her to insult Lara earlier had been replaced by pale-faced uncertainty . “I’m not. But it’s worth — ” Rapid, distant gunfire echoed through the sky. Lara, along with most of the crowd, looked northeast. In that moment, she realized what she’d always known but refused to acknowledge — Ronin wasn’t invincible, and she’d almost lost him too many times already . Please. Please let him be okay . “We must go, Lara.” Newton touched her arm as he came up beside her . Swallowing thickly, she nodded to him. “The fight just started,” she called to the crowd, “but the choice is yours. Will you battle for your freedom, or will you die in the dirt under Warlord’s boot ?” Chapter Thirty- Three T he lights of Cheyenne — so out of place in the pitch-black wasteland that had once been Wyoming — illuminated a swath of the overcast night sky. Ronin recognized the truth of the town, now; Cheyenne wasn’t a beacon of hope and security, but a monument to Warlord’s power and prejudice. The light was visible from miles around, projecting the challenge — I have what you do not; try to take from me so I may destroy you and claim what was yours , too . Outwardly, it was an impregnable fortress, a stronghold none could stand against. Ronin had single-handedly ended scores of reavers out in the Dust; surely, Warlord’s gearheads could handle even more from so defensible a position . But, despite its imposing appearance, Warlord’s wall was ineffective. Alpha Team, led by Ronin and Sergeant Maul, had crossed the nothingness between the base and Cheyenne and walked directly into the wall’s shadow, unopposed. The irregularly-placed guard towers on the north wall were empty. Ronin couldn’t guess what Warlord had tasked his gearheads with to leave their security so lax, but it didn’t matter . If everything went as planned, all the gearheads would be drawn out soon enough . Ronin stood beside Maul in the darkness beneath the wall, optics straying southwest. Somewhere, just under a mile-and-a-half distant, Lara and Bravo Team were arriving on the outskirts of the slums. Though Alpha Team was larger, Captain Cooper and some of his most experienced soldiers were with Lara . “Time to get our sorry asses over this wall,” Maul said, slinging his rifle across his back. His was amongst the first faces Ronin had seen after arriving at the base — Maul had been one of the soldiers who escorted Ronin and Newton inside . The other bots, Ronin included, swung their weapons free of their hands and stepped up to the wall. The human soldiers formed a perimeter, some of them equipped with old night vision goggles . “Land gracefully, boys,” one of the humans whispered. A quiet snicker spread through the team . “Break a leg, fellas,” a bot replied . “Shut up.” Maul swept his optics over the group until they were silent. “I’m not in the mood for any new ventilation ports.” Turning back to the wall, he directed one of the synths to boost him up. Most of the bots could jump high enough to haul themselves over the ten-foot-tall barrier, but it would be too noisy . The last time Ronin crossed the wall, he’d been running. He thought he’d known true fear that day, that his concern for Lara had reached its peak, but the panic of their flight seemed so small and insignificant compared to what they faced afterward. Her death had always been a possibility, but he’d never acknowledged it. Not really . Before they left Cheyenne, he’d promised to keep her safe, and hadn’t doubted his ability to do
